events.description: Competency commission examined large number of Indians with exceedingly disastrous results
Female allottee given patent to remaining portion of her allotment (approximately 80 acres)
Immediate sale of 80-acre allotment for $6,000 following patent receipt
Unnamed allottee purchased home in Anadarko with land sale proceeds
Approximately 220 Indians disposed of land immediately upon receipt of fee patents at ridiculously low prices
Unnamed allottee voluntarily deposited $1500 with agency office held to her credit

financial_transactions.description: Sale of remaining allotment acreage (approximately 80 acres) at approximate actual worth
Voluntary deposit held to allottee's credit
Immediate sale of land upon fee patent receipt with contracts existing prior to patent issuance
Rental payments for leased allotment lands

correspondence.subject: Status of fee patent Indians and competency commission examinations
correspondence.action_requested: Report on conditions of patent holders
correspondence.outcome: Information provided regarding 220 land sales and 25 retained allotments

correspondence.subject: Patent issuance policy and specific allotment case
correspondence.action_requested: Oppose wholesale patent issuance; consider each case on merits regardless of blood quantum
correspondence.outcome: Recommendation concurred; one patent issued direct to female allottee

correspondence.subject: Conditions of Cheyenne and Arapaho Indians and need for legislative action
correspondence.action_requested: Legislative action to force able-bodied Indians to work their lands
